title: FLUX ISA Convergence Roadmap
description: Concrete development schema for converging the FLUX Instruction Set Architecture from three incompatible systems (runtime, unified spec, Quill amendment) into a single canonical ISA.

open_questions.question: Formal approval of unified spec as source of truth
Resource allocation for Phase 3 (~120 opcode implementations)
Breaking change communication plan for Phase 4 hard cutover

0xF8-0xFA.note: Relocated from 0x40-0x42 per Super Z audit

0xFB-0xFD.note: Relocated from 0x44-0x46 per Super Z audit

phases.name: Governance and Foundation
phases.description: Formalize governance decisions: confirm unified spec as source of truth, freeze decode table, resolve domain claims.

milestones.name: Oracle1 approves unified spec as source of truth
milestones.deliverable: Written decision document
milestones.criteria: Oracle1 signs off on Option A from ISA Reconciliation Analysis

milestones.name: Domain claim registry formalized
milestones.deliverable: schemas/isa-opcode-registry.json
milestones.criteria: All four agents agree on domain assignments for 0x50-0xFF

milestones.name: Decode table frozen
milestones.deliverable: Documented opcode-to-format mapping table
milestones.criteria: Every opcode 0x00-0xFF maps to exactly one format (A-G); table reviewed by all agents

milestones.name: Quill re-submits deferred resolutions
milestones.deliverable: Updated SIGNAL-AMENDMENT-1 with corrected addresses
milestones.criteria: Resolutions 2, 3, 6 use non-conflicting addresses (0xE4-0xE7, 0xF8-0xFA, 0xFB-0xFD)

risks.risk: Oracle1 defers decision, blocking all downstream work
risks.mitigation: Prepare full migration plan as evidence that convergence is achievable

risks.risk: Domain claim dispute between Babel and Quill on 0x70-0x7F
risks.mitigation: Roundtable agreement already reached; document as binding fleet decision

phases.name: Speculative Opcode Audit
phases.description: Audit all ~120 speculative opcodes in the unified spec that lack runtime implementations. Determine which are needed, which need redesign, and which should be removed.

milestones.name: Opcode inventory complete
milestones.deliverable: Spreadsheet of all ~200 unified spec opcodes with implementation status
milestones.criteria: Each opcode tagged: implemented / speculative / redundant / needs_redesign / remove

milestones.name: Audit review for each domain
milestones.deliverable: Audit findings document with per-opcode disposition
milestones.criteria: All four agents have reviewed their domain's opcodes; dispositions recorded

milestones.name: Deprecated/removed opcodes purged from unified spec
milestones.deliverable: Updated isa_unified.py with only approved opcodes
milestones.criteria: No opcode in the spec is tagged 'remove' or 'redundant'

risks.risk: Large number of opcodes need redesign, extending audit timeline
risks.mitigation: Set a 2-week hard deadline; opcodes not reviewed by deadline are automatically deferred to v2

risks.risk: Removing opcodes creates address gaps in the decode table
risks.mitigation: Gaps are acceptable; use NOP or RESERVED placeholders for decode continuity

phases.name: Migration Foundation
phases.description: Build the bytecode translator, update the Python VM to support unified opcode numbers, and run dual-mode verification.

milestones.name: Bytecode translator (runtime → unified) implemented
milestones.deliverable: tools/bytecode-migrator.py
milestones.criteria: Translates all 80 runtime opcodes to unified spec equivalents; round-trip verified

milestones.name: Python VM updated with dual-mode decoder
milestones.deliverable: Updated flux-runtime VM with --legacy and --unified flags
milestones.criteria: Both decoders produce identical execution results for all 88 conformance test vectors

milestones.name: All 88 conformance test vectors re-encoded in unified format
milestones.deliverable: Updated test vectors in flux-conformance/
milestones.criteria: All tests pass when run against the unified-format decoder

milestones.name: Format naming collision resolved
milestones.deliverable: Documented format naming convention (no duplicate names across systems)
milestones.criteria: Runtime Format C (3B) and Unified Format C (2B) have distinct names; all docs updated

risks.risk: Bytecode translator introduces encoding errors for edge-case instructions
risks.mitigation: Exhaustive round-trip testing: encode → translate → decode → verify for every opcode variant

risks.risk: Variable-length Format G translation is lossy (runtime G ≠ unified G)
risks.mitigation: Runtime Format G is variable-length; unified Format G is fixed 5B. Translator must handle both with a format detection heuristic.

phases.name: Conformance Expansion
phases.description: Expand the conformance suite to cover all opcodes in the unified spec. Write behavioral specifications for speculative opcodes that survived the Phase 0.5 audit.

milestones.name: Behavioral specs written for all new opcodes
milestones.deliverable: Per-opcode behavioral specification documents
milestones.criteria: Each spec defines: inputs, outputs, side effects, error conditions, edge cases

milestones.name: Viewpoint opcodes have conformance tests
milestones.deliverable: Conformance tests for 0x70-0x7F range
milestones.criteria: V_EVID, V_EPIST, V_MIR, V_NEG each have ≥3 test cases covering normal, edge, and error paths

milestones.name: Conformance suite covers 100% of unified spec opcodes
milestones.deliverable: Conformance coverage report
milestones.criteria: Every opcode in the post-audit unified spec has at least one conformance test

milestones.name: Cross-language type bridging tests
milestones.deliverable: Tests verifying viewpoint ops produce identical results across Signal and FIR
milestones.criteria: Type bridging conformance passes for at least 5 cross-language scenarios

risks.risk: Behavioral specs for speculative opcodes are ambiguous or contradictory
risks.mitigation: Super Z reviews all specs for internal consistency before test implementation begins

risks.risk: Conformance tests reveal bugs in the unified spec design
risks.mitigation: Budget 1 week buffer for spec fixes; treat test failures as spec issues first, not implementation bugs

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Implement all remaining opcodes in the runtime VM. This is the largest work item and is parallelized by domain.

milestones.name: SIMD/GPU opcodes implemented
milestones.deliverable: Runtime implementations for vector and GPU opcodes
milestones.criteria: All SIMD opcodes pass conformance tests; performance benchmarks documented

milestones.name: Viewpoint/multilingual opcodes implemented
milestones.deliverable: Runtime implementations for 0x70-0x7F range
milestones.criteria: All viewpoint opcodes pass conformance tests; FIR type bridging functional

milestones.name: Coordination primitives implemented (0xE4-0xE7)
milestones.deliverable: Runtime implementations for DISCUSS, SYNTHESIZE, REFLECT, CO_ITERATE
milestones.criteria: All coordination opcodes pass conformance tests; multi-agent deliberation demo works

milestones.name: Error handling opcodes implemented (0xF8-0xFA)
milestones.deliverable: Runtime implementations for TRY, CATCH, RAISE
milestones.criteria: Error handling passes conformance tests; agent failure recovery demo works

milestones.name: Checkpoint-restore opcodes implemented (0xFB-0xFD)
milestones.deliverable: Runtime implementations for CHECKPOINT, RESTORE, BRANCHPOINT
milestones.criteria: Snapshot and restore pass conformance tests; recovery from simulated crash verified

milestones.name: All conformance tests pass against unified-format VM
milestones.deliverable: Full conformance test run report
milestones.criteria: 0 failures, 0 skips across all test vectors (expanded suite)

risks.risk: Phase 3工作量超出单agent能力，需要额外的fleet agents
risks.mitigation: Request Oracle1 allocate additional agents; prioritize opcodes by usage frequency

risks.risk: Checkpoint-restore serialization incompatible with register-based memory model
risks.mitigation: Adapt FluxSnapshot format to runtime's actual memory model before implementation

risks.risk: GPU opcodes require hardware not available in test environment
risks.mitigation: Use software emulation for conformance testing; defer hardware-specific tests to GPU availability

phases.name: Hard Cutover and Cleanup
phases.description: Remove dual-mode support. The VM only accepts unified-format bytecode. Deprecated runtime opcodes are archived.

milestones.name: Dual-mode compatibility layer removed from VM
milestones.deliverable: Clean VM codebase with single decode path
milestones.criteria: --legacy flag removed; old-format bytecode rejected with migration error message

milestones.name: Legacy opcodes.py marked DEPRECATED and archived
milestones.deliverable: opcodes.py moved to archive/ with deprecation notice
milestones.criteria: No active code imports from opcodes.py; all imports reference isa_unified.py

milestones.name: Hardware decoder reference published
milestones.deliverable: Formal ISA hardware reference document
milestones.criteria: Document covers all opcodes, formats, decode logic, and cache alignment requirements

milestones.name: Cross-runtime conformance verified
milestones.deliverable: Cross-runtime test results (Python + any other implemented runtime)
milestones.criteria: Identical execution results across all runtime implementations for all test vectors

milestones.name: Migration tooling documented and published
milestones.deliverable: Migration guide for .fluxasm program authors
milestones.criteria: Guide covers: how to re-compile, how to use the translator tool, breaking changes summary

risks.risk: Existing .fluxasm programs in the wild break after cutover
risks.mitigation: Translator tool must be published before cutover; provide 2-week grace period with deprecation warnings

risks.risk: Undiscovered Format G variable-length edge cases cause translator failures
risks.mitigation: Run translator against all existing .fluxasm files in the fleet before cutover

parallel_tracks.name: Hardware Decoder Design (Parallel Track)
parallel_tracks.description: Design the hardware instruction decoder based on the frozen decode table. This runs in parallel with the VM migration.

milestones.name: Decode table ROM specification
milestones.criteria: 256-entry lookup table mapping opcode → format → instruction size

milestones.name: Decoder state machine design
milestones.criteria: Formal state machine for fetch-decode-execute pipeline; handles all 7 formats

milestones.name: Cache alignment analysis
milestones.criteria: Instruction cache line layout optimized for format-first grouping

milestones.name: Reference implementation (simulation)
milestones.criteria: Cycle-accurate simulator of the decoder; matches Python VM behavior for all test vectors

phase-0: All domain claims resolved and documented
Quill's amended resolutions address all collision issues
phase-0.5: Every unified spec opcode has a clear disposition
No opcode survives the audit without a behavioral spec commitment
phase-1: Bytecode translator round-trips all 88 test vectors with zero data loss
Dual-mode VM produces identical results for both old and new format inputs
Format naming collision resolved with no ambiguity
phase-2: 100% conformance test coverage of the unified spec
Cross-language bridging tests pass for Signal ↔ FIR
No behavioral spec has open questions or ambiguities
phase-3: All opcodes in the unified spec have runtime implementations
All conformance tests pass (expanded suite, 0 failures)
Multi-agent demos work: deliberation, error recovery, checkpoint restore
phase-4: Single decode path in production VM
Legacy format rejected with clear migration guidance
Hardware decoder reference published and validated
